How do I import marketplace → registry (import direction) in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: incoming listing from the marketplace; produce a registry item.
;; ROUTE: four-part adapter with explicit provenance.

(define (marketplace-listing->registry-item listing)
  ;; 1. READ SOURCE — extract the fields we care about; ignore unknowns.
  (let ((listing-id (cdr (assq 'listing_id listing)))
        (title (cdr (assq 'title listing)))
        (desc (cdr (assq 'description listing)))
        (price-str (cdr (assq 'price listing)))
        (currency-code (cdr (assq 'currency_code listing)))
        (qty (cdr (assq 'quantity listing)))
        (raw-tags (cdr (assq 'tags listing)))
        (state-str (cdr (assq 'state listing)))
        (created (cdr (assq 'created_timestamp listing)))
        (modified (cdr (assq 'last_modified listing)))
        (images (cdr (assq 'images listing))))

    ;; Validate the pieces we require.
    (cond
      ((not (and listing-id title price-str))
         ;; Refuse loudly; the listing is not complete enough to import.
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'argument-missing)
               (cons 'human
                 "Marketplace listing missing required fields (id, title, or price).")
               (cons 'provenance
                     (list (cons 'by 'marketplace-listing->registry-item)
                           (cons 'source-had-fields (map car listing))))))
      (else
        ;; 2. NORMALIZE — coerce to canonical types.
        (let ((price-cents (dollars-string->cents price-str))
              (currency (currency-code->symbol currency-code))
              (tags-canonical (canonicalize-tags raw-tags))
              (state-sym (state-string->symbol state-str))
              (item-id-sym (listing-id->item-id listing-id)))

          (cond
            ((not price-cents)
               (list (cons 'refused #t)
                     (cons 'reason 'argument-malformed)
                     (cons 'human
                       (string-append "Could not parse price '" price-str "'."))
                     (cons 'provenance
                           (list (cons 'by 'marketplace-listing->registry-item)
                                 (cons 'source-price price-str)))))
            (else
              ;; 3. PROJECT TARGET — build the registry item shape.
              (let ((registry-item
                      (list
                        (cons 'item-id item-id-sym)
                        (cons 'title title)
                        (cons 'description desc)
                        (cons 'price-cents price-cents)
                        (cons 'currency currency)
                        (cons 'quantity qty)
                        (cons 'tags tags-canonical)
                        (cons 'state state-sym)
                        (cons 'created-at created)
                        (cons 'last-modified modified)
                        (cons 'image-urls images)
                        (cons 'source-market 'craft-market)
                        (cons 'source-listing-id listing-id))))
                ;; 4. PROVENANCE — an audit trail for the translation.
                (list
                  (cons 'payload registry-item)
                  (cons 'provenance
                        (list (cons 'by 'marketplace-listing->registry-item)
                              (cons 'from-market 'craft-market)
                              (cons 'source-listing-id listing-id)
                              (cons 'at (current-time-seconds))
                              (cons 'normalizations
                                    '(dollars->cents currency->symbol
                                      tags->symbols state->symbol
                                      listing-id->item-id-symbol)))))))))))))

;; Helpers.

(define (dollars-string->cents s)
  ;; "24.00" -> 2400. Returns #f on malformed input.
  (let ((n (string->number s)))
    (if n
        (round (* n 100))
        #f)))

(define (currency-code->symbol code)
  (cond
    ((equal? code "USD") 'usd)
    ((equal? code "EUR") 'eur)
    ((equal? code "GBP") 'gbp)
    ((equal? code "CAD") 'cad)
    (else 'unknown-currency)))

(define (canonicalize-tags tags)
  ;; Lowercase, strip whitespace, convert to symbols, dedupe.
  (let loop ((in tags) (out '()) (seen '()))
    (cond
      ((null? in) (reverse out))
      (else
        (let ((sym (string->symbol (car in))))
          (if (member sym seen)
              (loop (cdr in) out seen)
              (loop (cdr in)
                    (cons sym out)
                    (cons sym seen))))))))

(define (state-string->symbol s)
  (cond
    ((equal? s "active") 'active)
    ((equal? s "draft") 'draft)
    ((equal? s "sold_out") 'sold-out)
    ((equal? s "inactive") 'inactive)
    (else 'unknown-state)))

(define (listing-id->item-id id)
  (string->symbol (string-append "listing-" id)))

(define (current-time-seconds) 1725609600)
```

The read step, the normalize step, the project step, the provenance step.
How do I write an adapter for registry → marketplace (export direction) in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: local registry item changed; push the update to the marketplace.
;; ROUTE: read the registry item, project to marketplace shape,
;;        refuse if a required marketplace-only field is missing.

(define (registry-item->marketplace-update item)
  ;; 1. READ SOURCE.
  (let ((item-id (cdr (assq 'item-id item)))
        (title (cdr (assq 'title item)))
        (desc (cdr (assq 'description item)))
        (price-cents (cdr (assq 'price-cents item)))
        (currency (cdr (assq 'currency item)))
        (qty (cdr (assq 'quantity item)))
        (tags (cdr (assq 'tags item)))
        (state (cdr (assq 'state item)))
        (source-listing-id (cdr (assq 'source-listing-id item))))

    (cond
      ((not source-listing-id)
         ;; This item was never imported from the marketplace, so we do not
         ;; have a listing_id to update. Refuse rather than creating a new one.
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'argument-missing)
               (cons 'human
                 "Registry item has no source-listing-id; cannot map to a marketplace update.")
               (cons 'suggest '(create-new-listing))
               (cons 'provenance
                     (list (cons 'by 'registry-item->marketplace-update)
                           (cons 'item-id item-id)))))
      (else
        ;; 2. NORMALIZE — nothing to change; the registry IS our canonical form.
        ;; 3. PROJECT TARGET — coerce back to marketplace conventions.
        (let ((update-payload
                (list
                  (cons 'listing_id source-listing-id)
                  (cons 'title title)
                  (cons 'description desc)
                  (cons 'price (cents->dollars-string price-cents))
                  (cons 'currency_code (currency-symbol->code currency))
                  (cons 'quantity qty)
                  (cons 'tags (symbols->tag-strings tags))
                  (cons 'state (state-symbol->string state)))))
          ;; 4. PROVENANCE.
          (list
            (cons 'payload update-payload)
            (cons 'provenance
                  (list (cons 'by 'registry-item->marketplace-update)
                        (cons 'from-registry item-id)
                        (cons 'to-market 'craft-market)
                        (cons 'target-listing-id source-listing-id)
                        (cons 'at (current-time-seconds))
                        (cons 'normalizations
                              '(cents->dollars-string currency-symbol->code
                                symbols->tag-strings state-symbol->string))))))))))

(define (cents->dollars-string cents)
  (let ((dollars (quotient cents 100))
        (rem (remainder cents 100)))
    (string-append
      (number->string dollars)
      "."
      (if (< rem 10)
          (string-append "0" (number->string rem))
          (number->string rem)))))

(define (currency-symbol->code sym)
  (cond
    ((eq? sym 'usd) "USD")
    ((eq? sym 'eur) "EUR")
    ((eq? sym 'gbp) "GBP")
    ((eq? sym 'cad) "CAD")
    (else "USD")))  ;; safe default; the CAD case is refused upstream if problematic

(define (symbols->tag-strings tags)
  (map symbol->string tags))

(define (state-symbol->string sym)
  (cond
    ((eq? sym 'active) "active")
    ((eq? sym 'draft) "draft")
    ((eq? sym 'sold-out) "sold_out")
    ((eq? sym 'inactive) "inactive")
    (else "draft")))
```

The reverse. Same discipline; different shape mapping.
How do I import an Etsy adapter in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: sync operation — pull all live listings, import to registry.
;; ROUTE: fetch; for each, adapt; upsert; return report.

(define (sync/pull-and-import)
  (let ((listings (fetch-live-listings)))
    (cond
      ((not listings)
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'service-not-yet-wired)
               (cons 'human "Marketplace search unavailable; nothing pulled.")))
      (else
        (let ((results
                (map
                  (lambda (l)
                    (let ((adapted (marketplace-listing->registry-item l)))
                      (cond
                        ((refused? adapted)
                           (list (cons 'listing-id (cdr (assq 'listing_id l)))
                                 (cons 'imported #f)
                                 (cons 'refusal adapted)))
                        (else
                          (let ((item (cdr (assq 'payload adapted))))
                            (upsert-registry-item item)
                            (list (cons 'listing-id (cdr (assq 'listing_id l)))
                                  (cons 'imported #t)
                                  (cons 'item-id (cdr (assq 'item-id item)))))))))
                  listings)))
          (let ((imported (count-imported results))
                (refused (- (length results) (count-imported results))))
            (surface :say "Pulled ~a listings; imported ~a; refused ~a."
                     (length listings) imported refused)
            (list
              (cons 'total (length listings))
              (cons 'imported imported)
              (cons 'refused refused)
              (cons 'per-listing results)
              (cons 'provenance
                    (list (cons 'by 'sync/pull-and-import)
                          (cons 'at (current-time-seconds)))))))))))

(define (sync/push-changes changed-items)
  (map
    (lambda (item)
      (let ((update (registry-item->marketplace-update item)))
        (cond
          ((refused? update)
             (list (cons 'item-id (cdr (assq 'item-id item)))
                   (cons 'pushed #f)
                   (cons 'refusal update)))
          (else
            (let ((payload (cdr (assq 'payload update))))
              (send-marketplace-update payload)
              (list (cons 'item-id (cdr (assq 'item-id item)))
                    (cons 'pushed #t)
                    (cons 'listing-id (cdr (assq 'listing_id payload)))))))))
    changed-items))

(define (fetch-live-listings)
  ;; Stand-in for a metered call to the marketplace API.
  '())

(define (upsert-registry-item item)
  ;; Stand-in for a shop registry write.
  #t)

(define (send-marketplace-update payload)
  ;; Stand-in for a metered call to the marketplace API.
  #t)

(define (count-imported results)
  (length (filter (lambda (r) (cdr (assq 'imported r))) results)))

(define (refused? x)
  (and (list? x) (assq 'refused x) (cdr (assq 'refused x))))
```

Now compose. She writes a `sync/pull-and-import` and a `sync/push-changes`, each of which loops over listings and calls the adapter.

```motoi
(define week/summary-lite
  (register-verb
    'week/summary-lite
    (list
      (cons 'implementation do-week-summary-lite)
      (cons 'signature '(week/summary-lite))
      (cons 'returns 'summary-descriptor-or-refusal)
      (cons 'library 'week)
      (cons 'kind 'mut)
      (cons 'cost 0)                            ;; free — no external call
      (cons 'quota-tier 'imagine)
      (cons 'stability 'stable))))

(define (do-week-summary-lite)
  ;; A heuristic summary: top three most-frequent event types + top item.
  (let ((events (last-week-events)))
    (let ((by-type (group-by-type events)))
      (let ((top-three (take (sort-by-count by-type) 3)))
        (list
          (cons 'summary (format-lite-summary top-three))
          (cons 'method 'frequency-based)
          (cons 'provenance
                (list (cons 'by 'week/summary-lite)
                      (cons 'method 'heuristic))))))))

(define (group-by-type events) events)
(define (sort-by-count xs) xs)
(define (take xs n) xs)
(define (format-lite-summary xs) "three sentence lite summary")
```

```motoi
;; SITUATION: define the promise shape and a few examples.
;; ROUTE: data-first; predicates as lambdas; suggestions as symbols.

(define example-promises
  (list
    (list
      (cons 'name 'floor-price)
      (cons 'statement "I will not accept less than $8 for any item.")
      (cons 'predicate (lambda (proposal)
                         (>= (or (cdr (assq 'price proposal)) 0) 8)))
      (cons 'violation-message
        (lambda (proposal)
          (string-append
            "Proposed price is $"
            (number->string (or (cdr (assq 'price proposal)) 0))
            "; my floor is $8.")))
      (cons 'suggest '(price-up-to-floor))
      (cons 'authored-at 1720000000))

    (list
      (cons 'name 'isbn-required)
      (cons 'statement "I will not list a book without an ISBN.")
      (cons 'predicate (lambda (proposal)
                         (or (not (eq? (cdr (assq 'kind proposal)) 'book))
                             (assq 'isbn proposal))))
      (cons 'violation-message
        (lambda (proposal)
          "Proposed book listing has no ISBN."))
      (cons 'suggest '(book/isbn-lookup))
      (cons 'authored-at 1720500000))

    (list
      (cons 'name 'no-early-open)
      (cons 'statement "I will not open the shop before 9 AM.")
      (cons 'predicate (lambda (proposal)
                         (or (not (eq? (cdr (assq 'kind proposal)) 'shop-open))
                             (>= (cdr (assq 'hour proposal)) 9))))
      (cons 'violation-message
        (lambda (proposal)
          (string-append
            "Proposed open time is "
            (number->string (cdr (assq 'hour proposal)))
            " AM; my floor is 9 AM.")))
      (cons 'suggest '(wait-until-9am))
      (cons 'authored-at 1721000000))))
```

How do I add a verb for a refusal verb in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: check a proposal against all promises.
;; ROUTE: iterate; first violation refuses; empty violations lets through.

(define (do-promise-check proposal)
  (let ((violations (find-violations proposal (registered-promises))))
    (cond
      ((null? violations)
         ;; Pass through with provenance recording which promises were checked.
         (list
           (cons 'passed #t)
           (cons 'checked (length (registered-promises)))
           (cons 'provenance
                 (list (cons 'by 'shop/promise-check)
                       (cons 'promises-checked
                             (map (lambda (p) (cdr (assq 'name p)))
                                  (registered-promises)))))))
      (else
        ;; Refuse with the FIRST violated promise's full context.
        (let ((first-v (car violations)))
          (let ((promise (cdr (assq 'promise first-v)))
                (msg-fn (cdr (assq 'violation-message first-v))))
            (list
              (cons 'refused #t)
              (cons 'reason 'invariant-violated)
              (cons 'human (msg-fn proposal))
              (cons 'promise (cdr (assq 'name promise)))
              (cons 'promise-statement (cdr (assq 'statement promise)))
              (cons 'suggest (cdr (assq 'suggest promise)))
              (cons 'other-violations
                    (map (lambda (v)
                           (cdr (assq 'name (cdr (assq 'promise v)))))
                         (cdr violations)))
              (cons 'provenance
                    (list (cons 'by 'shop/promise-check)
                          (cons 'violated (cdr (assq 'name promise)))
                          (cons 'proposal proposal))))))))))

(define (find-violations proposal promises)
  (filter
    (lambda (p)
      (let ((pred (cdr (assq 'predicate p))))
        (not (pred proposal))))
    (map
      (lambda (p)
        (list (cons 'promise p)
              (cons 'violation-message (cdr (assq 'violation-message p)))))
      promises)))

(define (registered-promises)
  ;; In real code, this reads from a cortex or a config file.
  example-promises)
```

Iterate the promises; test each; collect violations; produce refusal-with-help.

```motoi
;; SITUATION: check-then-do wrappers so every consequential action honors the promises.

(define (guarded-list-item item)
  (let ((proposal (list (cons 'kind 'list-item)
                        (cons 'price (cdr (assq 'price item)))
                        (cons 'item-kind (cdr (assq 'kind item)))
                        (cons 'isbn (cdr (assq 'isbn item))))))
    (let ((check (shop/promise-check proposal)))
      (cond
        ((refused? check)
           check)     ;; refusal propagates unchanged
        (else
          (shoppe/open)
          (do-actual-list item))))))

  (let ((proposal (list (cons 'kind 'shop-open) (cons 'hour hour))))
    (let ((check (shop/promise-check proposal)))
      (cond
        ((refused? check) check)
        (else (shoppe/open))))))

(define (refused? x)
  (and (list? x) (assq 'refused x) (cdr (assq 'refused x))))

(define (do-actual-list item) (list (cons 'listed item)))
```

How do I work with move 1: the dialect declaration in Motoi?

```motoi
;; bookshop-scheme/dialect.slat

(dialect
  :name "bookshop-scheme"
  :descends-from "sakura-curator"
  :version "0.1"
  :introduced-at 1725609600
  :author 'used-book-operator

  ;; --- REFUSES ---
  ;; Five parent verbs we do not carry. Each with a specific reason.
  :refuses (
    (:verb "shop/mark-perishable" :because "bookshops don't sell perishables")
    (:verb "shop/rotate-stock"    :because "books do not rotate")
    (:verb "shop/expiry-warn"     :because "books have no expiry")
    (:verb "shop/publish-print-on-demand" :because "out of scope for used-book operations")
    (:verb "shop/produce-listing-blitz"   :because "our marketing is one-listing-at-a-time"))

  ;; --- MODIFIES ---
  ;; Three parent verbs with altered semantics.
  :modifies (
    (:verb "shop/list-item"
     :change "adds required :isbn slot for books; refuses without it"
     :parent-signature "(shop/list-item item)"
     :fork-signature "(shop/list-item item :isbn isbn-13)"
     :migration "caller must supply :isbn; adapter attempts ISBN lookup")
    (:verb "shop/tag"
     :change "tag vocabulary narrowed to Dewey-adjacent set"
     :parent-signature "(shop/tag item tags)"
     :fork-signature "(shop/tag item bookshop-tags)"
     :migration "adapter maps parent tags to bookshop-tags where possible")
    (:verb "shop/estimate-price"
     :change "uses ISBN-based comparables instead of image-based"
     :parent-signature "(shop/estimate-price item)"
     :fork-signature "(shop/estimate-price item :condition condition-symbol)"
     :migration "caller must supply :condition; adapter defaults to 'good"))

  ;; --- ADOPTS ---
  ;; The rest of Sakura-Curator carries unchanged.
  :adopts "all-not-refused-not-modified"

  ;; --- ADDS ---
  ;; Twelve new verbs specific to used-book operations.
  :adds (
```

How do I write an adapter for move 2: the adapter in Motoi?

```motoi
;; bookshop-scheme/adapters/to-sakura-curator.slat

(adapter
  :from "bookshop-scheme"
  :to "sakura-curator"

  :translations (

    (:verb "shop/list-item"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda (item . kws)
         (let ((isbn (get-keyword-arg kws ':isbn #f)))
           (list
             (cons 'call '(shop/list-item))
             (cons 'args (list item))
             (cons 'provenance
                   (list (cons 'dropped :isbn)
                         (cons 'isbn-was isbn)
                         (cons 'reason "parent does not track :isbn"))))))
     :parent->fork
       (lambda (item)
         (let ((isbn (attempt-isbn-lookup item)))
           (if isbn
               (list (cons 'call '(shop/list-item))
                     (cons 'args (list item :isbn isbn))
                     (cons 'provenance (list (cons 'looked-up isbn))))
               (list (cons 'refused #t)
                     (cons 'reason 'argument-missing)
                     (cons 'human "bookshop-scheme requires :isbn for shop/list-item.")
                     (cons 'suggest '(operator-supply-isbn)))))))

    (:verb "shop/tag"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda (item bookshop-tags)
         (list (cons 'call '(shop/tag))
               (cons 'args (list item bookshop-tags))))
     :parent->fork
       (lambda (item parent-tags)
         (let ((approved (filter bookshop-approved-tag? parent-tags))
               (dropped (filter (lambda (t) (not (bookshop-approved-tag? t)))
                                parent-tags)))
           (list (cons 'call '(shop/tag))
                 (cons 'args (list item approved))
                 (cons 'provenance (list (cons 'dropped-tags dropped)))))))

    (:verb "shop/estimate-price"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda (item . kws)
         (let ((cond-sym (get-keyword-arg kws ':condition 'good)))
           (list (cons 'call '(shop/estimate-price))
                 (cons 'args (list item))
                 (cons 'provenance (list (cons 'dropped :condition)
                                         (cons 'condition-was cond-sym))))))
     :parent->fork
       (lambda (item)
         (list (cons 'call '(shop/estimate-price))
               (cons 'args (list item :condition 'good))
               (cons 'provenance (list (cons 'assumed 'good-condition)))))))

  :refusals (
    (:verb "shop/mark-perishable"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme")
               (cons 'human "bookshop-scheme does not carry perishable-goods verbs."))))
    (:verb "shop/rotate-stock"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme")
               (cons 'human "bookshop-scheme does not rotate stock; books do not spoil."))))
    (:verb "shop/expiry-warn"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme"))))
    (:verb "shop/publish-print-on-demand"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme"))))
    (:verb "shop/produce-listing-blitz"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme")))))

  :additions (
    ;; For fork-only verbs, parent contexts get a refusal.
    ;; Twelve entries elided — same shape as below.
    (:verb "book/isbn-lookup"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'fork-specific)
               (cons 'human "book/isbn-lookup is bookshop-scheme-specific.")
               (cons 'suggest '(run-under-fork-runtime)))))))

(define (get-keyword-arg args key default)
  (cond
    ((null? args) default)
    ((null? (cdr args)) default)
    ((eq? (car args) key) (cadr args))
    (else (get-keyword-arg (cdr args) key default))))

(define (attempt-isbn-lookup item) #f)

(define (bookshop-approved-tag? tag)
  (member tag
    '(fiction non-fiction reference biography history poetry drama
      children juvenile young-adult adult mystery science-fiction
      fantasy romance thriller horror memoir essay travel cookbook
      art music textbook philosophy religion self-help)))
```

Bidirectional. Handled explicitly per modified verb.

```motoi
(define (do-close-reconcile txns)
  (let ((shop-total (apply + (map txn-amount txns)))
        (payment-total (fetch-payment-total-today)))
    (let ((delta (- shop-total payment-total)))
      (cond
        ((zero? delta)
           (list (cons 'reconciled #t) (cons 'delta 0)))
        ((< (abs delta) 0.02)
           (list (cons 'reconciled 'approx) (cons 'delta delta)))
        (else
           (list (cons 'refused #t)
                 (cons 'reason 'invariant-violated)
                 (cons 'human
                   (string-append "Reconciliation delta of $"
                                  (number->string delta)
                                  " — operator review needed."))
                 (cons 'suggest '(operator-review-transactions))))))))
```

```motoi
(define (do-close-tag-anomalies txns)
  (let ((n (length txns))
        (auth (afford (list (cons 'reason 'close-anomaly-scan)
                            (cons 'count n)
                            (cons 'total-cents (* n 1))))))
    (cond
      ((not auth)
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'operator-declined)
               (cons 'human "Anomaly scan skipped; closeout continues.")))
      (else
         (let ((tagged
                 (map (lambda (t)
                        (list (cons 'txn t)
                              (cons 'flag (ai/classify
                                            (list (cons 'type 'txn-anomaly)
                                                  (cons 'input t))))))
                      txns)))
           (list (cons 'tagged tagged)
                 (cons 'anomaly-count
                       (length (filter (lambda (row)
                                         (not (eq? (cdr (assq 'flag row)) 'normal)))
                                       tagged)))))))))

(define close/tag-anomalies
  (register-verb 'close/tag-anomalies
    (list (cons 'implementation do-close-tag-anomalies)
          (cons 'library 'shoppe)
          (cons 'cost 1)
          (cons 'metered-because '(external-wire-call classification))
          (cons 'surface-hook 'confirm-if-batch)
          (cons 'stability 'unstable))))
```

How do I work with lists for anchor: a photo-to-listing adapter in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator has a photo of a vintage camera; wants a listing image.
;; ROUTE: read the phone photo, normalize to a square RGB frame,
;;        project to the marketplace's expected shape, keep provenance.

(define (photo->listing-image photo-descriptor marketplace)
  ;; 1. Read the source. We accept the photo descriptor as-is.
  (let ((source-w (cdr (assq 'width photo-descriptor)))
        (source-h (cdr (assq 'height photo-descriptor)))
        (source-bytes (cdr (assq 'bytes photo-descriptor)))
        (source-taken-at (cdr (assq 'taken-at photo-descriptor))))
    ;; 2. Normalize to an intermediate: a square RGB frame at a canonical size.
    (let ((short-edge (min source-w source-h))
          (canonical-size 1600))
      (let ((intermediate
              (list (cons 'shape 'square)
                    (cons 'edge short-edge)
                    (cons 'target-edge canonical-size)
                    (cons 'pixels source-bytes)
                    (cons 'color-space 'srgb))))
        ;; 3. Project to the target: whatever the marketplace wants.
        (let ((target-spec (marketplace-image-spec marketplace)))
          (let ((projected
                  (list
                    (cons 'width  (cdr (assq 'width  target-spec)))
                    (cons 'height (cdr (assq 'height target-spec)))
                    (cons 'format (cdr (assq 'format target-spec)))
                    (cons 'quality (cdr (assq 'quality target-spec)))
                    (cons 'from intermediate))))
            ;; 4. Preserve provenance. Where did this come from? When? Under what rules?
            (list
              (cons 'payload projected)
              (cons 'provenance
                    (list
                      (cons 'source 'phone-camera)
                      (cons 'taken-at source-taken-at)
                      (cons 'adapted-by 'photo->listing-image)
                      (cons 'target marketplace))))))))))

(define (marketplace-image-spec marketplace)
  (cond
    ((eq? marketplace 'craft-market)
       (list (cons 'width 1200) (cons 'height 1200)
             (cons 'format 'jpeg) (cons 'quality 82)))
    ((eq? marketplace 'auction-house)
       (list (cons 'width 1600) (cons 'height 1200)
             (cons 'format 'jpeg) (cons 'quality 90)))
    ((eq? marketplace 'social-feed)
       (list (cons 'width 1080) (cons 'height 1080)
             (cons 'format 'jpeg) (cons 'quality 85)))
    (else
       (list (cons 'width 1024) (cons 'height 1024)
             (cons 'format 'jpeg) (cons 'quality 80)))))

;; Example run.
(define example-photo
  (list (cons 'width 4032)
        (cons 'height 3024)
        (cons 'bytes 'phone-jpeg-bytes-placeholder)
        (cons 'taken-at 1721128800)))

(define adapted (photo->listing-image example-photo 'craft-market))

(display "Adapted payload: ")
(display (cdr (assq 'payload adapted)))
(newline)
(display "Provenance: ")

(newline)
```

How do I `match` on depth: a listing-to-listing adapter in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator updated a listing on one marketplace.
;; ROUTE: adapt the listing to the target marketplace's expected shape.
;; The source and target are BOTH marketplace listings; the intermediate is our own.

(define (listing->listing source-listing source-market target-market)
  ;; 1. Read: extract what we care about from the source shape.
  (let ((title (cdr (assq 'title source-listing)))
        (price (cdr (assq 'price source-listing)))
        (qty   (cdr (assq 'quantity source-listing)))
        (desc  (cdr (assq 'description source-listing)))
        (tags  (cdr (assq 'tags source-listing)))
        (photos (cdr (assq 'photos source-listing))))
    ;; 2. Normalize to our intermediate: the plain-shop-item shape.
    (let ((intermediate
            (list
              (cons 'title title)
              (cons 'price-cents (dollars->cents price))
              (cons 'quantity qty)
              (cons 'description-plain (strip-marketplace-formatting desc source-market))
              (cons 'tags-canonical (canonicalize-tags tags))
              (cons 'photos photos))))
      ;; 3. Project to the target's expected shape.
      (let ((projected (project-to-market intermediate target-market)))
        ;; 4. Provenance: what did we do to it, and why.
        (list
          (cons 'payload projected)
          (cons 'provenance
                (list
                  (cons 'source-market source-market)
                  (cons 'target-market target-market)
                  (cons 'adapted-by 'listing->listing)
                  (cons 'normalizations
                        (list 'price-to-cents 'strip-formatting 'canonicalize-tags)))))))))

(define (dollars->cents price)
  ;; price is a real number of dollars; return integer cents.
  (round (* price 100)))

(define (strip-marketplace-formatting text market)
  ;; Some marketplaces embed HTML; some use their own markup.
  ;; The intermediate is plain text. We do not try to preserve formatting here.
  (cond
    ((eq? market 'craft-market) text)      ;; already plain
    ((eq? market 'auction-house) text)     ;; already plain
    (else text)))

(define (canonicalize-tags tags)
  ;; Normalize case, dedupe, remove empties.
  (let ((seen '()))
    (let loop ((in tags) (out '()))
      (if (null? in)
          (reverse out)
          (let ((t (car in)))
            (if (or (equal? t "")
                    (member t seen))
                (loop (cdr in) out)
                (begin
                  (set! seen (cons t seen))
                  (loop (cdr in) (cons t out)))))))))

(define (project-to-market intermediate market)
  (let ((title (cdr (assq 'title intermediate)))
        (cents (cdr (assq 'price-cents intermediate)))
        (qty (cdr (assq 'quantity intermediate)))
        (desc (cdr (assq 'description-plain intermediate)))
        (tags (cdr (assq 'tags-canonical intermediate)))
        (photos (cdr (assq 'photos intermediate))))
    (cond
      ((eq? market 'craft-market)
         (list (cons 'title title)
               (cons 'price (/ cents 100.0))
               (cons 'inventory qty)
               (cons 'description desc)
               (cons 'tags (limit-tags tags 13))
               (cons 'images photos)))
      ((eq? market 'auction-house)
         (list (cons 'title title)
               (cons 'starting-bid (/ cents 100.0))
               (cons 'quantity qty)
               (cons 'description desc)
               (cons 'category-hints (limit-tags tags 5))
               (cons 'photos photos)))
      (else
         (list (cons 'title title)
               (cons 'price (/ cents 100.0))
               (cons 'stock qty)
               (cons 'body desc)
               (cons 'tags tags)
               (cons 'images photos))))))

(define (limit-tags tags n)
  (if (or (null? tags) (<= n 0))
      '()
      (cons (car tags) (limit-tags (cdr tags) (- n 1)))))
```

An operator maintains three shop presences. Sometimes they update one listing and want the others to match.
How do I write an adapter for depth: a keystroke-to-card adapter in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator presses a key while a card is focused.
;; ROUTE: adapt the raw input event into a card action, or ignore.

(define (keystroke->card-action event focused-card)
  ;; 1. Read the source: the input event carries a key symbol and modifiers.
  (let ((key (cdr (assq 'key event)))
        (mods (cdr (assq 'modifiers event)))
        (at (cdr (assq 'timestamp event))))
    ;; 2. Normalize: what does this keystroke MEAN, independent of card?
    (let ((intent (keystroke->intent key mods)))
      (if (eq? intent 'no-op)
          #f
          ;; 3. Project: what does this intent DO on the focused card?
          (let ((action (intent->card-action intent focused-card)))
            (if action
                ;; 4. Provenance: the card action carries where it came from.
                (list
                  (cons 'action action)
                  (cons 'target focused-card)
                  (cons 'provenance
                        (list (cons 'via 'keystroke)
                              (cons 'key key)
                              (cons 'mods mods)
                              (cons 'at at))))
                #f))))))

(define (keystroke->intent key mods)
  (cond
    ((and (eq? key 'return) (null? mods)) 'commit)
    ((and (eq? key 'escape) (null? mods)) 'cancel)
    ((and (eq? key 'space) (null? mods))  'toggle)
    ((and (eq? key 'tab) (null? mods))    'next-field)
    ((and (eq? key 'slash) (null? mods))  'search)
    (else 'no-op)))

(define (intent->card-action intent card)
  (let ((kind (cdr (assq 'kind card))))
    (cond
      ((and (eq? intent 'commit) (eq? kind 'form))
         'form-submit)
      ((and (eq? intent 'commit) (eq? kind 'list))
         'list-select)
      ((and (eq? intent 'toggle) (eq? kind 'switch))
         'switch-flip)
      ((eq? intent 'cancel)
         'card-dismiss)
      ((eq? intent 'search)
         'open-search)
      (else #f))))
```

Adapters do not only bridge network services. They also bridge input events to cards on the surface — a keystroke to a card action, a tap to a shop verb, a voice utterance to an intent.

How do I write an adapter for depth: a voice-utterance-to-cart adapter in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator says "restock the blue mugs to twenty."
;; ROUTE: adapt the utterance to a cart proposal, but do not fire it —
;;        firing is the operator's decision (see Chapter 12 on metered verbs).

(define (utterance->cart-proposal utterance shop-context)
  (let ((tokens (tokenize utterance)))
    ;; 2. Normalize: extract the intent + slot values.
    (let ((parsed (parse-shop-utterance tokens shop-context)))
      (if (not parsed)
          (list (cons 'kind 'unparsed) (cons 'utterance utterance))
          (let ((intent (cdr (assq 'intent parsed)))
                (slots (cdr (assq 'slots parsed))))
            ;; 3. Project: intent + slots become a cart proposal.
            (let ((proposal (intent->cart-proposal intent slots shop-context)))
              ;; 4. Provenance.
              (list
                (cons 'proposal proposal)
                (cons 'provenance
                      (list (cons 'via 'voice)
                            (cons 'utterance utterance)
                            (cons 'intent intent)
                            (cons 'slots slots))))))))))

(define (tokenize s)
  ;; Simple word split. Real code lowercases + strips punctuation first.
  (string-split s " "))

(define (parse-shop-utterance tokens ctx)
  (cond
    ((match-prefix? tokens '("restock" "the"))
       (let ((rest (list-tail tokens 2)))
         (let ((product (find-known-product rest (cdr (assq 'products ctx))))
               (qty (find-number rest)))
           (if (and product qty)
               (list (cons 'intent 'restock)
                     (cons 'slots (list (cons 'product product)
                                        (cons 'quantity qty))))
               #f))))
    ((match-prefix? tokens '("price" "check"))
       (let ((rest (list-tail tokens 2)))
         (let ((product (find-known-product rest (cdr (assq 'products ctx)))))
           (if product
               (list (cons 'intent 'price-check)
                     (cons 'slots (list (cons 'product product))))
               #f))))
    (else #f)))

(define (match-prefix? tokens prefix)
  (cond
    ((null? prefix) #t)
    ((null? tokens) #f)
    ((equal? (car tokens) (car prefix))
       (match-prefix? (cdr tokens) (cdr prefix)))
    (else #f)))

(define (find-known-product tokens known)
  ;; Return the first token that matches a known product name.
  (cond
    ((null? tokens) #f)
    ((member (car tokens) known) (car tokens))
    (else (find-known-product (cdr tokens) known))))

(define (find-number tokens)
  ;; Return the first token that parses as a number, else #f.
  ;; The word "twenty" would need a small word->number table in real code.
  (cond
    ((null? tokens) #f)
    (else
      (let ((n (string->number (car tokens))))
        (if n n (find-number (cdr tokens)))))))

(define (intent->cart-proposal intent slots ctx)
  (cond
    ((eq? intent 'restock)
       (list (cons 'cart-kind 'restock)
             (cons 'product (cdr (assq 'product slots)))
             (cons 'target-quantity (cdr (assq 'quantity slots)))
             (cons 'estimated-cost 0)))  ;; free — this is a shop-registry write
    ((eq? intent 'price-check)
       (list (cons 'cart-kind 'price-check)
             (cons 'product (cdr (assq 'product slots)))
             (cons 'estimated-cost 3)))  ;; small — hits an external price API
    (else #f)))
```

Same shape again, larger surface. Voice utterances arrive as raw text from a transcription service; the adapter turns them into a cart proposal.
How do I add a verb for anchor: a verb born and registered in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator wants a verb that opens the shop AND checks disputes.
;; ROUTE: (1) propose the shape, (2) register the name, (3) document,
;;        (4) stabilize by pinning behavior, (5) plan for retirement.

;; 1. Propose. Author the body first as a plain define. This is not yet a verb.
(define (proposed-shop-open-with-check)
  (let ((pending (shoppe/transactions)))
    (let ((disputes (filter (lambda (t)
                              (eq? (cdr (assq 'status t)) 'disputed))
                            pending)))
      (if (null? disputes)
          (shoppe/open)
          (list (cons 'refused 'has-disputes)
                (cons 'count (length disputes))
                (cons 'suggest 'address-disputes-first))))))

;; 2. Register. This is the step that makes the name public and callable
;;    by the operator's other scripts. The registration is where the
;;    metering, permission, and lifecycle metadata attach.
(define shop/open-with-check
  (register-verb
    'shop/open-with-check
    (list
      (cons 'implementation proposed-shop-open-with-check)
      (cons 'signature '(shop/open-with-check))
      (cons 'returns 'shop-state-or-refusal)
      (cons 'library 'shoppe)
      (cons 'kind 'mut)
      (cons 'cost 0)
      (cons 'stability 'unstable)   ;; new verbs start unstable
      (cons 'introduced-at 1721128800)
      (cons 'author 'operator))))

;; 3. Document. The doc is the promise. Written the day you register.
(document-verb 'shop/open-with-check
  (list
    (cons 'summary
      "Open the shop only if there are no pending disputes.")
    (cons 'explanation
      "Reads pending transactions, filters to disputed status, opens the shop \
       if none are disputed. If any are disputed, returns a refusal descriptor \
       naming the count and suggesting the operator address disputes first.")
    (cons 'examples
      (list
        (list (cons 'tier 'novice)
              (cons 'code "(shop/open-with-check)")
              (cons 'note "Open, or return refusal."))))
    (cons 'caveats
      (list
        "Refusal is a data value, not an error; check the returned shape."
        "Reads all pending transactions; O(n) in transaction count."))
    (cons 'related '(shoppe/open shoppe/transactions))))

;; 4. Stabilize. After the operator has used it for a while and no bugs
;;    surfaced, they mark it stable. Now other operators may depend on it.
(stabilize-verb 'shop/open-with-check
  (list (cons 'promoted-at 1723807200)
        (cons 'note "Two weeks of daily use, no changes needed.")))

;; 5. Retire. Not today; but the shape of retirement is planned now.
;; When shoppe/transactions eventually adds a :status filter, this verb
;; becomes redundant. At that point, we deprecate before removing.
```

Here is the smallest complete birth of a new verb. An operator wants a shortcut for opening their shop with a check for pending disputes.
How do I add a verb for depth: adapting a verb without breaking it in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: shop/open-with-check needs a :force keyword so an operator
;; can open despite disputes if they are handling them out-of-band.
;; ROUTE: version the verb; keep the old shape callable; document both.

;; Original shape:
;;   (shop/open-with-check) -> state-or-refusal

;; New shape:
;;   (shop/open-with-check [:force? #t/#f]) -> state-or-refusal

(define (shop-open-with-check-v2 . args)
  (let ((force? (get-keyword-arg args ':force? #f)))
    (let ((pending (shoppe/transactions)))
      (let ((disputes (filter (lambda (t)
                                (eq? (cdr (assq 'status t)) 'disputed))
                              pending)))
        (cond
          (force?
             (shoppe/open))
          ((null? disputes)
             (shoppe/open))
          (else
             (list (cons 'refused 'has-disputes)
                   (cons 'count (length disputes))
                   (cons 'suggest 'address-disputes-first))))))))

(define (get-keyword-arg args key default)
  (cond
    ((null? args) default)
    ((null? (cdr args)) default)
    ((eq? (car args) key) (cadr args))
    (else (get-keyword-arg (cdr args) key default))))

;; Re-register with a new implementation, bumping the version.
(re-register-verb 'shop/open-with-check
  (list
    (cons 'implementation shop-open-with-check-v2)
    (cons 'signature '(shop/open-with-check :force? boolean))
    (cons 'returns 'shop-state-or-refusal)
    (cons 'version 2)
    (cons 'compatible-with 1)   ;; the old zero-arg call still works
    (cons 'changed-at 1725609600)
    (cons 'change-note "Added :force? keyword; zero-arg behavior unchanged.")))

;; Update the doc to reflect the new shape.
(document-verb 'shop/open-with-check
  (list
    (cons 'summary
      "Open the shop; refuse (or force through) if disputes are pending.")
    (cons 'signature '(shop/open-with-check [:force? #t/#f]))
    (cons 'examples
      (list
        (list (cons 'tier 'novice)
              (cons 'code "(shop/open-with-check)")
              (cons 'note "Open if no disputes."))
        (list (cons 'tier 'intermediate)
              (cons 'code "(shop/open-with-check :force? #t)")
              (cons 'note "Open even if disputes are pending."))))
    (cons 'caveats
      (list
        "With :force? #t, the disputes remain — you have opted to handle them elsewhere."))
    (cons 'related '(shoppe/open shoppe/transactions))))
```

The interesting part of a lifecycle is not birth or death. It is growth.
How do I add a verb for depth: deprecating a verb in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: an early verb `shop/quick-open` bypassed the dispute check
;; entirely. That was a mistake. We want it gone, but two operators call it.
;; ROUTE: deprecate now, remove later, tell the callers where to look next.

(deprecate-verb 'shop/quick-open
  (list
    (cons 'reason "Bypassed dispute check; shop/open-with-check :force? #t is the honest replacement.")
    (cons 'replacement 'shop/open-with-check)
    (cons 'replacement-call '(shop/open-with-check :force? #t))
    (cons 'warn-from 1725609600)      ;; today
    (cons 'remove-at  1733385600)     ;; three months out
    (cons 'grace-period-days 90)))

;; During the grace period, the verb still runs but prints a warning
;; on every call. The warning names the replacement.
(define (warned-shop-quick-open)
  (surface :say
    "shop/quick-open is deprecated; use (shop/open-with-check :force? #t). \
     Removal scheduled for 2024-12-05.")
  (shoppe/open))

(re-register-verb 'shop/quick-open
  (list (cons 'implementation warned-shop-quick-open)
        (cons 'deprecated? #t)))
```

Sometimes a verb has to go — the promise it made no longer matches how the world works. Deprecation is a two-phase move: **warn**, then **remove**.

How do I sign depth: verb signature introspection in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator has authored a dozen verbs over months and wants
;; a report: which are stable, which are unstable, which are deprecated.
;; ROUTE: iterate the registered verbs; group by stability; show the counts.

(define (verb-survey)
  (let ((all (registered-verbs)))
    (let ((stable    (filter (lambda (v) (eq? (verb-stability v) 'stable)) all))
          (unstable  (filter (lambda (v) (eq? (verb-stability v) 'unstable)) all))
          (deprecated (filter (lambda (v) (verb-deprecated? v)) all)))
      (surface :say "Verb survey:")
      (surface :say "  stable:     ~a" (length stable))
      (surface :say "  unstable:   ~a" (length unstable))
      (surface :say "  deprecated: ~a" (length deprecated))
      (for-each
        (lambda (v)
          (surface :say "    -> ~a  (remove ~a)"
                   (verb-name v)
                   (verb-remove-at v)))
        deprecated))))

(define (verb-stability v)
  (cdr (assq 'stability (verb-metadata v))))

(define (verb-deprecated? v)
  (cdr (assq 'deprecated? (verb-metadata v))))

(define (verb-remove-at v)
  (cdr (assq 'remove-at (verb-metadata v))))

(define (verb-name v)
  (cdr (assq 'name (verb-metadata v))))
```

How do I work with lists for verb lifecycles in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: Friday review — audit verbs, promote the deserving, deprecate the stale.
;; ROUTE: survey; per-verb decide; write the changes back.

(define (weekly-verb-review)
  (surface :say "---- Friday verb review ----")
  (verb-survey)
  (surface :say "")
  ;; Promote verbs that have been unstable for at least three weeks
  ;; AND have been called at least ten times.
  (let ((candidates (promotion-candidates)))
    (surface :say "~a verbs are ready to stabilize:" (length candidates))
    (for-each
      (lambda (v)
        (surface :say "  ~a  (used ~a times over ~a days)"
                 (verb-name v)
                 (verb-call-count v)
                 (verb-age-days v)))
      candidates))
  (surface :say "")
  ;; Suggest deprecation for verbs unused for two months.
  (let ((stale (staleness-candidates)))
    (surface :say "~a verbs unused for 60+ days:" (length stale))
    (for-each
      (lambda (v)
        (surface :say "  ~a  (last called ~a days ago)"
                 (verb-name v)
                 (verb-days-since-last-call v)))
      stale)))

(define (promotion-candidates)
  (filter
    (lambda (v)
      (and (eq? (verb-stability v) 'unstable)
           (>= (verb-age-days v) 21)
           (>= (verb-call-count v) 10)))
    (registered-verbs)))

(define (staleness-candidates)
  (filter
    (lambda (v)
      (and (not (verb-deprecated? v))
           (>= (verb-days-since-last-call v) 60)))
    (registered-verbs)))

(define (verb-call-count v)
  (cdr (assq 'call-count (verb-metadata v))))

(define (verb-age-days v)
  (cdr (assq 'age-days (verb-metadata v))))

(define (verb-days-since-last-call v)
  (cdr (assq 'days-since-last-call (verb-metadata v))))

;; The review does not itself promote or deprecate. It reports. The operator
;; decides. This is deliberate — automating the promotion decision would
;; occasionally promote a verb she was still uncertain about, and the promise
;; of stability is one no automation should make on her behalf.

;; Missing verb note: register-verb, document-verb, stabilize-verb,
;; deprecate-verb, re-register-verb, registered-verbs, verb-metadata,
;; promotion-candidates, staleness-candidates are runtime introspection
;; verbs. If they are not yet in the reference, they should be added under
;; a `runtime/` or `verb/` library. Missing verbs: register-verb,
;; re-register-verb, document-verb, stabilize-verb, deprecate-verb,
;; registered-verbs, verb-metadata — needs reference addition.
```

Show me a recursive example of anchor: a metered price-check verb in Motoi.

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator wants competitor price for a specific product query.
;; ROUTE: declare cost + quota; check before fire; return the price or a refusal.

;; The verb body. Notice: NO cost logic inside. The body only computes.
(define (do-competitor-price-check query)
  (let ((results (web/search query)))
    (if (not results)
        (list (cons 'refused 'search-empty))
        (let ((first (car results)))
          (let ((price (web/extract first "price")))
            (if price
                (list (cons 'price price)
                      (cons 'source (web/extract first "url")))
                (list (cons 'refused 'no-price-in-result))))))))

;; Registration attaches metering metadata. The runtime enforces.
(define shop/competitor-price-check
  (register-verb
    'shop/competitor-price-check
    (list
      (cons 'implementation do-competitor-price-check)
      (cons 'signature '(shop/competitor-price-check query))
      (cons 'returns 'price-descriptor-or-refusal)
      (cons 'library 'shoppe)
      (cons 'kind 'mut)

      ;; --- METERING METADATA ---
      (cons 'cost 3)                       ;; estimated pennies per call
      (cons 'cost-currency 'usd-cents)
      (cons 'quota-window 'daily)          ;; the operator's daily bucket
      (cons 'quota-tier 'imagine)          ;; minimum tier that can call this
      (cons 'surface-hook 'confirm-if-batch)
      (cons 'metered-because '(external-wire-call web-search))
      (cons 'stability 'stable))))

;; Now the operator calls it like any other verb. The runtime does the
;; cost check at dispatch. If the operator is out of quota, the verb
;; never enters its body — it returns a refusal descriptor instead.

(shop/competitor-price-check "vintage folding camera")
;; -> ((price . 42.00) (source . "https://example-market/item/123"))
;; -> or ((refused . out-of-daily-quota) (used . 200) (limit . 200))
```

How do I meter depth: a batch with pre-authorization in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: run competitor price check on all products in the shop.
;; ROUTE: batch the calls under a single authorization; surface the total.

(define (weekly-competitor-scan)
  (let ((products (shoppe/transactions)))       ;; stand-in for a product list
    (let ((n (length products))
          (per-call-cost 3))
      (let ((total-cents (* n per-call-cost)))
        ;; Ask the runtime to pre-authorize the whole batch.
        ;; If the operator declines, we do not fire any calls.
        ;; If the operator approves, subsequent calls in this scope
        ;; do not re-prompt.
        (let ((auth (afford (list
                              (cons 'reason 'weekly-competitor-scan)
                              (cons 'count n)
                              (cons 'per-call-cents per-call-cost)
                              (cons 'total-cents total-cents)
                              (cons 'summary
                                (format "Check competitor prices for ~a products (~a total)"
                                        n
                                        (cents->display total-cents)))))))
          (cond
            ((not auth)
               (list (cons 'refused 'operator-declined)))
            (else
               (let ((results
                       (map
                         (lambda (product)
                           (let ((query (product->query product)))
                             (shop/competitor-price-check query)))
                         products)))
                 (list (cons 'ran n)
                       (cons 'spent total-cents)
                       (cons 'results results))))))))))

(define (product->query product)
  ;; Turn a product record into a search string. Real code would use
  ;; title, brand, model. Simplified here.
  (cdr (assq 'title product)))

(define (cents->display c)
  ;; 320 cents -> "$3.20"
  (let ((dollars (quotient c 100))
        (rem (remainder c 100)))
    (string-append "$"
                   (number->string dollars)
                   "."
                   (if (< rem 10)
                       (string-append "0" (number->string rem))
                       (number->string rem)))))
```

```motoi
;; SITUATION: transcribe an audio clip; free below 60 seconds daily,
;;            small charge for each additional second.
;; ROUTE: register with tiered cost; runtime does the arithmetic.

(define (do-transcribe audio-clip)
  ;; The body computes. It does not know about tiers.
  (let ((text (audio/transcribe-with-cloud-help audio-clip)))
    (if text
        (list (cons 'text text)
              (cons 'duration-sec (audio-clip-duration audio-clip)))
        (list (cons 'refused 'transcription-empty)))))

(define (audio-clip-duration clip)
  (cdr (assq 'duration-sec clip)))

(define shop/transcribe
  (register-verb
    'shop/transcribe
    (list
      (cons 'implementation do-transcribe)
      (cons 'signature '(shop/transcribe audio-clip))
      (cons 'returns 'transcription-descriptor-or-refusal)
      (cons 'library 'shoppe)
      (cons 'kind 'mut)

      ;; --- TIERED METERING ---
      (cons 'cost-model 'tiered)
      (cons 'tiers
        (list
          (list (cons 'up-to-seconds 60) (cons 'cents-per-second 0))
          (list (cons 'up-to-seconds 600) (cons 'cents-per-second 1))
          (list (cons 'up-to-seconds 3600) (cons 'cents-per-second 2))))
      (cons 'cost-dimension 'duration-sec)
      (cons 'quota-window 'daily)
      (cons 'surface-hook 'silent-under-free-tier)
      (cons 'metered-because '(external-wire-call cloud-transcription))
      (cons 'stability 'stable))))
```

```motoi
;; SITUATION: operator asks for a deep-reasoning pass but is on the free tier.
;; ROUTE: refuse with tier info + upgrade path + cheaper alternative.

(define (do-deep-analyze detail)
  (afford/deep-think detail))

(define shop/deep-analyze
  (register-verb
    'shop/deep-analyze
    (list

      (cons 'signature '(shop/deep-analyze detail))
      (cons 'returns 'reasoning-descriptor-or-refusal)
      (cons 'library 'shoppe)
      (cons 'kind 'mut)
      (cons 'cost 50)                        ;; premium wire call
      (cons 'quota-tier 'magic)              ;; only magic tier can call
      (cons 'refuse-shape 'refusal-with-help)
      (cons 'cheaper-alternative 'shop/quick-analyze)
      (cons 'upgrade-path '(tier-imagine tier-dream tier-magic))
      (cons 'metered-because '(external-reasoning-model))
      (cons 'stability 'stable))))

;; When a lower-tier caller invokes shop/deep-analyze, the runtime's
;; refusal descriptor looks like this:

;; ((refused . out-of-tier)
;;  (need-tier . magic)
;;  (current-tier . imagine)
;;  (cheaper-alternative . shop/quick-analyze)
;;  (upgrade-path . (tier-imagine tier-dream tier-magic))
;;  (would-have-cost-cents . 50)
;;  (would-have-affected . reasoning-descriptor))
```

When a metered verb refuses, it should refuse with information — not *no* but *no, because, here is what would have happened, here is what to do instead*. A well-shaped refusal is a small piece of teaching.

How do I work with lists for common desktop task: the antique-dealer's batch price sweep in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: 40 items, 3 price sources, cost visible per source.
;; ROUTE: build a plan; show the totals; single authorization; batch.

(define (monday-price-sweep items)
  (let ((n (length items)))
    (let ((plan
            (list
              (list (cons 'source 'auction-archive)
                    (cons 'per-call 0)         ;; free — cached
                    (cons 'total 0))
              (list (cons 'source 'craft-market-listings)
                    (cons 'per-call 3)
                    (cons 'total (* n 3)))
              (list (cons 'source 'web-search)
                    (cons 'per-call 5)
                    (cons 'total (* n 5))))))
      (let ((grand-total (apply + (map (lambda (s) (cdr (assq 'total s))) plan))))
        ;; Show the operator the plan before firing anything.
        (surface :say "Monday price sweep — ~a items:" n)
        (for-each
          (lambda (source-plan)
            (surface :say "  ~a  ~a per call  ~a total"
                     (cdr (assq 'source source-plan))
                     (cents->display (cdr (assq 'per-call source-plan)))
                     (cents->display (cdr (assq 'total source-plan)))))
          plan)
        (surface :say "  ---")
        (surface :say "  grand total: ~a" (cents->display grand-total))
        ;; Ask once for the whole plan.
        (let ((auth (afford
                      (list (cons 'reason 'monday-price-sweep)
                            (cons 'grand-total-cents grand-total)
                            (cons 'plan plan)))))
          (cond
            ((not auth)
               (surface :say "Sweep declined; nothing was called.")
               (list (cons 'refused 'operator-declined)))
            (else
               ;; Run three passes in order. Cheapest first — that way,
               ;; if a later pass fails, the operator has partial data.
               (let ((archive-results (map auction-archive-lookup items))
                     (market-results (map craft-market-lookup items))
                     (web-results (map web-price-lookup items)))
                 (list
                   (cons 'items n)
                   (cons 'spent grand-total)
                   (cons 'archive archive-results)
                   (cons 'market market-results)
                   (cons 'web web-results)))))))
      )))

(define (auction-archive-lookup item)
  ;; This is a free verb; cached data.
  (list (cons 'item item)
        (cons 'source 'archive)
        (cons 'price (archive-price-of item))))

(define (craft-market-lookup item)
  ;; This is a metered verb. Runtime handles the debit under the
  ;; batch authorization we just secured.
  (shop/competitor-price-check (item->query item)))

(define (web-price-lookup item)
  ;; Also metered.
  (shop/competitor-price-check (item->query item)))

(define (archive-price-of item)
  ;; Stand-in for a local cache lookup.
  (or (cdr (assq 'archive-price item)) 'not-in-archive))

(define (item->query item)
  (cdr (assq 'title item)))
```

An antique-dealer operator inherits a lot of forty items on a Monday. She wants prices for all of them — cross-referenced against three sources: the auction-house archive, the craft-market current listings, and the world web.
How do I use Cortex for anchor: a three-book pipeline in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: on new transaction — chime, classify buyer, update dashboard plot.
;; ROUTE: pipeline three verbs from three books; carry provenance; sum cost.

(define (on-new-transaction txn)
  ;; Step 1: BOOK OF MUSIC — play a chime.
  ;;   Free verb; no metering; adds a music-provenance stamp.
  (radio/play 'chime-new-transaction)
  (let ((music-prov
          (list (cons 'played 'chime-new-transaction)
                (cons 'via 'radio/play))))

    ;; Step 2: BOOK OF CORTEX — is this buyer known?
    ;;   Metered verb (small cost); returns a classification.
    (let ((cortex-result
            (cortex/topk
              (list (cons 'query 'buyer-history)
                    (cons 'key (cdr (assq 'buyer-id txn)))
                    (cons 'k 5)))))
      (let ((cortex-prov
              (list (cons 'looked-up (cdr (assq 'buyer-id txn)))
                    (cons 'via 'cortex/topk)
                    (cons 'k 5))))

        ;; Step 3: BOOK OF SYSTEMS — update the dashboard plot.
        ;;   Free verb; adds a plot-provenance stamp.
        (let ((buyer-class (classify-from-cortex cortex-result)))
          (plot/bar
            (list (cons 'series 'buyer-mix)
                  (cons 'label buyer-class)
                  (cons 'delta 1)))
          (let ((plot-prov
                  (list (cons 'plotted 'buyer-mix)
                        (cons 'incremented buyer-class)
                        (cons 'via 'plot/bar))))
            ;; Return the composed result with all three provenances.
            (list
              (cons 'txn-id (cdr (assq 'id txn)))
              (cons 'buyer-class buyer-class)
              (cons 'provenance
                (list (cons 'music music-prov)
                      (cons 'cortex cortex-prov)
                      (cons 'plot plot-prov))))))))))

(define (classify-from-cortex results)
  ;; If the cortex found history, buyer is 'returning; otherwise 'new.
  (cond
    ((null? results) 'new)
    ((zero? (length results)) 'new)
    (else 'returning)))
```

An operator wants a verb that, when a new transaction arrives, plays a chime, asks the cortex whether this buyer is new or returning, and updates a plot. Three books; one pipeline.

```motoi
;; SITUATION: a new photo arrives; three books should each do their thing.
;;   Book of Systems: store metadata in the shop registry.
;;   Book of Cortex: embed the photo for later search.
;;   Book of Cine:   produce a thumbnail for the operator's review carousel.
;; ROUTE: fan out one photo to three verbs; collect three provenances.

(define (on-new-photo photo)
  (let ((for-shop (store-photo-metadata photo))
        (for-cortex (embed-photo-for-search photo))
        (for-cine (produce-review-thumb photo)))
    (list
      (cons 'photo-id (cdr (assq 'id photo)))
      (cons 'shop-result for-shop)
      (cons 'cortex-result for-cortex)
      (cons 'cine-result for-cine)
      (cons 'provenance
        (list (cons 'shop (cdr (assq 'provenance for-shop)))
              (cons 'cortex (cdr (assq 'provenance for-cortex)))
              (cons 'cine (cdr (assq 'provenance for-cine))))))))

(define (store-photo-metadata photo)
  ;; Free verb from Book of Systems.
  (let ((id (cdr (assq 'id photo))))
    (list (cons 'stored id)
          (cons 'provenance
            (list (cons 'via 'shop/store-photo-metadata))))))

(define (embed-photo-for-search photo)
  ;; Metered verb from Book of Cortex. Small cost; runtime handles.
  (let ((v (ai/embed (cdr (assq 'bytes photo)))))
    (list (cons 'embedded #t)
          (cons 'dim (vector-length v))
          (cons 'provenance
            (list (cons 'via 'ai/embed)
                  (cons 'cost-cents 1))))))

(define (produce-review-thumb photo)
  ;; Free verb from Book of Cine — image manipulation, no wire call.
  (let ((thumb (cine/frame photo)))
    (list (cons 'thumb thumb)
          (cons 'provenance
            (list (cons 'via 'cine/frame)
                  (cons 'target-size 320))))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of depth: the reduce pattern.

```motoi
;; SITUATION: should we auto-restock this product?
;;   Book of Systems (shop):   what's the current stock?
;;   Book of Ops (statistics): what's the recent sales velocity?
;;   Book of Cortex (memory):  when did we last restock this product?
;; ROUTE: three reads, one decision.

(define (should-auto-restock? product-id)
  ;; Book of Systems.
  (let ((stock (current-stock product-id))
        ;; Book of Ops (via stat/mean over the last N days).
        (velocity (recent-velocity product-id 14))
        ;; Book of Cortex.
        (last-restock (last-restock-timestamp product-id)))
    (let ((days-supply (if (> velocity 0)
                           (/ stock velocity)
                           1000))
          (days-since-restock (days-since last-restock)))
      ;; The reduce step: three inputs, one decision, transparent about which.
      (let ((decision
              (cond
                ((< days-supply 3) 'restock-urgent)
                ((and (< days-supply 7) (> days-since-restock 30)) 'restock-normal)
                ((< days-supply 14) 'restock-soon)
                (else 'no-restock-needed))))
        (list
          (cons 'decision decision)
          (cons 'inputs
            (list (cons 'stock stock)
                  (cons 'velocity velocity)
                  (cons 'days-supply days-supply)
                  (cons 'days-since-restock days-since-restock)))
          (cons 'provenance
            (list (cons 'shop 'current-stock)
                  (cons 'ops 'stat/mean-over-14-days)
                  (cons 'cortex 'last-restock-timestamp))))))))

(define (current-stock product-id)
  ;; Read from the shop registry.
  42)  ;; stand-in

(define (recent-velocity product-id days)
  ;; Fetch recent sales and average.
  (let ((sales-per-day (list 3 5 2 4 6 3 5 4 2 3 5 4 3 4)))
    (stat/mean sales-per-day)))

(define (last-restock-timestamp product-id)
  ;; Cortex lookup.
  (let ((results
          (cortex/topk (list (cons 'query 'restock-events)
                             (cons 'key product-id)
                             (cons 'k 1)))))
    (if (null? results)
        0
        (cdr (assq 'at (car results))))))

(define (days-since ts)
  (let ((now (current-time-seconds)))
    (quotient (- now ts) 86400)))

(define (current-time-seconds)
  1725609600)  ;; stand-in
```

Three books each produce a piece of a decision; a single reducer combines them into an answer. Reduce.
How do I handle errors around depth: the guard pattern in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: an operator's script wants to publish a batch to a marketplace.
;; ROUTE: guard on shop-open? and on-quota; then fan out to publish + plot + chime.

(define (guarded-batch-publish items)
  ;; Guard 1: is the shop open?
  (cond
    ((not (shop-open?))
       (list (cons 'refused 'shop-closed)))
    ;; Guard 2: is the operator within their daily publish quota?
    ((over-daily-publish-quota? (length items))
       (list (cons 'refused 'over-publish-quota)
             (cons 'requested (length items))
             (cons 'remaining (remaining-publish-quota))))
    (else
      ;; All guards passed; do the composition.
      (let ((publish-results (map publish-one items))
            (plot-result (plot/bar
                           (list (cons 'series 'daily-publishes)
                                 (cons 'delta (length items))))))
        (radio/play 'chime-batch-published)
        (list
          (cons 'published (length items))
          (cons 'per-item publish-results)
          (cons 'plot plot-result)
          (cons 'provenance
            (list (cons 'guarded-by '(shop-open? within-publish-quota))
                  (cons 'via '(publish-one plot/bar radio/play)))))))))

(define (shop-open?)
  (cdr (assq 'open (shoppe/balance))))
```

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: an operator's script wants to publish a batch to a marketplace.
;; ROUTE: guard on shop-open? and on-quota; then fan out to publish + plot + chime.

(define (guarded-batch-publish items)
  ;; Guard 1: is the shop open?
  (cond
    ((not (shop-open?))
       (list (cons 'refused 'shop-closed)))
    ;; Guard 2: is the operator within their daily publish quota?
    ((over-daily-publish-quota? (length items))
       (list (cons 'refused 'over-publish-quota)
             (cons 'requested (length items))
             (cons 'remaining (remaining-publish-quota))))
    (else
      ;; All guards passed; do the composition.
      (let ((publish-results (map publish-one items))
            (plot-result (plot/bar
                           (list (cons 'series 'daily-publishes)
                                 (cons 'delta (length items))))))
        (radio/play 'chime-batch-published)
        (list
          (cons 'published (length items))
          (cons 'per-item publish-results)
          (cons 'plot plot-result)
          (cons 'provenance
            (list (cons 'guarded-by '(shop-open? within-publish-quota))
                  (cons 'via '(publish-one plot/bar radio/play)))))))))

(define (shop-open?)
  (cdr (assq 'open (shoppe/balance))))

(define (over-daily-publish-quota? n)
  (> (+ n (todays-publish-count)) (max-daily-publishes)))

(define (todays-publish-count)
  20)  ;; stand-in

(define (max-daily-publishes)
  50)  ;; stand-in

(define (remaining-publish-quota)
  (- (max-daily-publishes) (todays-publish-count)))

(define (publish-one item)
  ;; Metered — one wire call per item. Runtime handles.
  (list (cons 'item-id (cdr (assq 'id item)))
        (cons 'published #t)))
```

How do I use Cortex for common desktop task: the vintage-camera seller's morning routine in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: five things across four books, one script, once each morning.
;; ROUTE: guarded pipeline; single authorization for the metered step; provenance.

(define (morning-routine today)
  ;; Step 1 (guard): actually the operator's morning? (Skip on weekends.)
  (if (weekend? today)
      (list (cons 'skipped 'weekend))
      (begin
        ;; Step 2: BOOK OF SYSTEMS — check overnight messages.
        (let ((messages (check-overnight-messages)))
          (surface :say "~a new messages overnight." (length messages))

          ;; Step 3: BOOK OF SYSTEMS — new photos to embed.
          (let ((photos (list-new-photos-since-last-morning)))
            (surface :say "~a new photos." (length photos))

            ;; Step 4: BOOK OF CORTEX (metered) — batch embed.
            (let ((embed-cost (* (length photos) 1)))
              (if (> embed-cost 0)
                  (let ((auth (afford
                                (list (cons 'reason 'morning-embed)
                                      (cons 'count (length photos))
                                      (cons 'total-cents embed-cost)))))
                    (if auth
                        (for-each embed-photo-for-search photos)
                        (surface :say "Embed skipped; operator declined.")))
                  #f))

            ;; Step 5: BOOK OF MUSIC — opening chime.
            (radio/play 'chime-morning-open)

            ;; Step 6: BOOK OF SYSTEMS — sparklines.
            (update-dashboard-sparklines)

            ;; Step 7: BOOK OF CORTEX (metered, premium tier) — Monday-only summary.
            (if (monday? today)
                (let ((summary-cost 50))
                  (let ((auth (afford
                                (list (cons 'reason 'monday-week-summary)
                                      (cons 'total-cents summary-cost)))))
                    (if auth
                        (let ((summary (ai/summarize (last-week-events))))
                          (surface :say "Week summary:")
                          (surface :say "  ~a" summary))
                        (surface :say "Weekly summary skipped; operator declined."))))
                #f)

            (list
              (cons 'ran 'morning-routine)
              (cons 'messages-checked (length messages))
              (cons 'photos-embedded (length photos))
              (cons 'summary-attempted (monday? today))))))))

(define (weekend? day)
  (or (eq? day 'saturday) (eq? day 'sunday)))

(define (monday? day)
  (eq? day 'monday))

(define (check-overnight-messages)
  (list))  ;; stand-in

(define (list-new-photos-since-last-morning)
  (list))  ;; stand-in

(define (update-dashboard-sparklines)
  (plot/bar (list (cons 'series 'daily-open))))

(define (last-week-events)
  (list))  ;; stand-in
```
