How do I use SLAT for depth: an adapter between fork and parent in Motoi?

```motoi
;; bookshop-scheme/adapters/to-sakura-curator.slat
;; Translates calls in both directions between bookshop-scheme and sakura-curator.
;; No LLM required; deterministic table lookup + shape rewrites.

(adapter
  :from "bookshop-scheme"
  :to "sakura-curator"

  ;; Handle each modified verb explicitly.
  :translations (

    ;; shop/list-item: fork requires :isbn; parent does not know about it.
    ;; When translating fork->parent, we drop the :isbn (but preserve it
    ;; in provenance so the audit trail is intact).
    (:verb "shop/list-item"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda (item . kws)
         (let ((isbn (get-keyword-arg kws ':isbn #f)))
           (list
             (cons 'call '(shop/list-item))
             (cons 'args (list item))
             (cons 'provenance
                   (list (cons 'dropped :isbn)
                         (cons 'isbn-was isbn)
                         (cons 'reason "parent does not carry :isbn"))))))
     :parent->fork
       (lambda (item)
         ;; No :isbn from the parent call. We attempt lookup; if we cannot
         ;; find one, we refuse rather than list without an ISBN.
         (let ((isbn (attempt-isbn-lookup item)))
           (if isbn
               (list (cons 'call '(shop/list-item))
                     (cons 'args (list item :isbn isbn))
                     (cons 'provenance
                           (list (cons 'looked-up isbn))))
               (list (cons 'refused 'no-isbn-found)
                     (cons 'suggest 'operator-supply-isbn))))))

    ;; shop/tag: fork constrains to Dewey-adjacent tags.
    (:verb "shop/tag"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda (item bookshop-tags)
         ;; Widen bookshop-tags to the parent's more general tag set.
         ;; Trivial: bookshop-tags is a subset.
         (list (cons 'call '(shop/tag))
               (cons 'args (list item bookshop-tags))))
     :parent->fork
       (lambda (item parent-tags)
         ;; Narrow parent-tags to the bookshop-approved set. Some may
         ;; be dropped; we record what we lost.
         (let ((approved (filter bookshop-approved-tag? parent-tags))
               (dropped (filter (lambda (t) (not (bookshop-approved-tag? t)))
                                parent-tags)))
           (list (cons 'call '(shop/tag))
                 (cons 'args (list item approved))
                 (cons 'provenance
                       (list (cons 'dropped-tags dropped))))))))

  ;; Refused verbs: any parent call to a refused verb returns a refusal.
  :refusals (
    (:verb "shop/mark-perishable"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme")
               (cons 'reason "bookshop-scheme does not carry perishable-goods verbs"))))
    (:verb "shop/rotate-stock"
     :parent->fork
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused 'not-in-fork)
               (cons 'fork "bookshop-scheme")
               (cons 'reason "bookshop-scheme does not carry stock-rotation verbs")))))

  ;; Added verbs: parent has no idea what these are. When a fork script
  ;; invokes an added verb from a parent context, adapter returns an
  ;; unrecognized-verb refusal with the fork name attached.
  :additions (
    (:verb "book/isbn-lookup"
     :fork->parent
       (lambda args
         (list (cons 'refused 'fork-specific)
               (cons 'suggest 'run-under-fork-runtime))))))

(define (attempt-isbn-lookup item)
  ;; Real code would consult a local cache first, then a cheap wire call.
  ;; Stand-in returns #f.
  #f)

(define (bookshop-approved-tag? tag)
  (member tag
    '(fiction non-fiction reference biography history poetry drama
      children juvenile young-adult adult mystery science-fiction
      fantasy romance thriller horror memoir essay travel cookbook
      art music textbook philosophy religion self-help)))

(define (get-keyword-arg args key default)
  (cond
    ((null? args) default)
    ((null? (cdr args)) default)
    ((eq? (car args) key) (cadr args))
    (else (get-keyword-arg (cdr args) key default))))
```

The dialect.slat says *what changed.* The adapter says *how to translate.* Here is the adapter for `bookshop-scheme` reaching back to `sakura-curator`.
How do I use SLAT for depth: the bookshop runtime prologue in Motoi?

```motoi
;; bookshop-scheme/runtime/prologue.slat
;; Read at fork startup. Produces the fork's effective verb table.

(define (fork-prologue parent-reference-path fork-dialect-path)
  ;; 1. Load parent's reference — the full set of ancestor verbs.
  (let ((parent-verbs (read-reference parent-reference-path)))
    ;; 2. Load fork's dialect declaration.
    (let ((dialect (read-dialect fork-dialect-path)))
      (let ((refused-names (map cadr (get-field dialect ':refuses)))
            (modified (get-field dialect ':modifies))
            (adopted-names (get-field dialect ':adopts))
            (added (get-field dialect ':adds)))
        ;; 3. Build the effective table.
        ;;    - drop refused
        ;;    - swap in modified signatures
        ;;    - carry over adopted (default)
        ;;    - add new
        (let ((table (make-hash-table)))
          ;; Start with parent verbs; drop refused; keep adopted.
          (for-each
            (lambda (verb)
              (let ((name (cdr (assq 'name verb))))
                (cond
                  ((member name refused-names)
                     ;; skip; refused
                     #f)
                  ((assoc name (map (lambda (m) (cadr m)) modified))
                     ;; modified; will be replaced below
                     (hash-table-set! table name (mark-modified verb)))
                  (else
                     ;; adopted (or implicitly carried)
                     (hash-table-set! table name verb)))))
            parent-verbs)
          ;; Apply modifications: replace the parent's version.
          (for-each
            (lambda (mod)
              (let ((name (cadr mod)))
                (hash-table-set! table name (build-modified-verb mod))))
            modified)
          ;; Add new verbs.
          (for-each
            (lambda (add-decl)
              (let ((name (cdr (assq ':verb (cdr add-decl)))))
                (hash-table-set! table name (build-added-verb add-decl))))
            added)
          ;; Return the effective table + a manifest.
          (list
            (cons 'table table)
            (cons 'manifest
                  (list
                    (cons 'from "parent") (cons 'count (length parent-verbs))
                    (cons 'refused (length refused-names))
                    (cons 'modified (length modified))
                    (cons 'added (length added))
                    (cons 'effective (hash-table-size table))))))))))

(define (read-reference path)
  ;; Stand-in — real code parses the SLAT reference file.
  '())

(define (read-dialect path)
  '())

(define (get-field record field)
  (cdr (assq field record)))

(define (mark-modified verb)
  (cons (cons 'modified? #t) verb))

(define (build-modified-verb mod)
  (list (cons 'name (cadr mod))
        (cons 'modified? #t)
        (cons 'fork-signature (cadr (assq ':fork-signature (cddr mod))))))

(define (build-added-verb add-decl)
  (list (cons 'name (cadr (assq ':verb (cdr add-decl))))
        (cons 'added? #t)
        (cons 'signature (cadr (assq ':signature (cdr add-decl))))))
```

When a fork boots, it reads the parent's reference AND its own dialect.slat, applies the refusals, modifications, and additions, and produces the effective verb table for its runtime.
How do I add a verb for the fork protocol in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: fork day — author dialect.slat, spin up the runtime,
;;            translate her existing scripts, publish.
;; ROUTE: five steps, done once, then she lives in her own dialect.

(define (fork-day)
  (surface :say "Fork day — bookshop-scheme spinning up.")

  ;; Step 1: author dialect.slat.
  (write-dialect-file "bookshop-scheme/dialect.slat"
    (bookshop-dialect-declaration))
  (surface :say "  1. dialect.slat authored.")

  ;; Step 2: author the adapter to the parent.
  (write-adapter-file "bookshop-scheme/adapters/to-sakura-curator.slat"
    (bookshop-to-parent-adapter))
  (surface :say "  2. adapter to parent authored.")

  ;; Step 3: run the prologue; see the effective verb count.
  (let ((prologue-result (fork-prologue "sakura-curator/reference.slat"
                                        "bookshop-scheme/dialect.slat")))
    (let ((manifest (cdr (assq 'manifest prologue-result))))
      (surface :say "  3. runtime prologue done:")
      (surface :say "       refused: ~a  modified: ~a  added: ~a  effective: ~a"
               (cdr (assq 'refused manifest))
               (cdr (assq 'modified manifest))
               (cdr (assq 'added manifest))
               (cdr (assq 'effective manifest)))))

  ;; Step 4: translate her existing scripts.
  (let ((translated (translate-scripts "bookshop-scheme/my-scripts/"
                                        "bookshop-scheme/adapters/to-sakura-curator.slat")))
    (surface :say "  4. scripts translated: ~a of ~a clean, ~a need review."
             (cdr (assq 'clean translated))
             (cdr (assq 'total translated))
             (cdr (assq 'needs-review translated))))

  ;; Step 5: publish the fork's reference so other bookshop operators can adopt.
  (publish-reference "bookshop-scheme/docs/reference.slat")
  (surface :say "  5. reference published.")

  (surface :say "Fork day done. You now live in bookshop-scheme."))

(define (write-dialect-file path content) #f)   ;; stand-in
(define (write-adapter-file path content) #f)
(define (translate-scripts dir adapter) (list (cons 'clean 12)
                                              (cons 'total 14)
                                              (cons 'needs-review 2)))
(define (publish-reference path) #f)
(define (bookshop-dialect-declaration) '())
(define (bookshop-to-parent-adapter) '())
```

How do I write a refusal for anchor: a well-shaped refusal in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: a verb that reads from a shop registry needs to refuse
;;            when the shop is closed, but usefully.
;; ROUTE: return a refusal descriptor with reason, scope, alternative,
;;        provenance, and (optionally) retry hints.

(define (read-shop-inventory)
  (cond
    ((not (shop-open?))
       ;; The well-shaped refusal.
       (list
         ;; 1. REFUSED — a distinguishing marker so callers can pattern-match.
         (cons 'refused #t)
         ;; 2. REASON — a symbol callers can dispatch on.
         (cons 'reason 'shop-closed)
         ;; 3. HUMAN-MESSAGE — one sentence the operator can read.
         (cons 'human "Shop is closed for the day.")
         ;; 4. SUGGEST — an alternative call, or a next-step verb.
         (cons 'suggest '(shop/open))
         ;; 5. RETRY-AFTER — when it might make sense to try again.
         (cons 'retry-after 'next-shop-open-time)
         ;; 6. PROVENANCE — where this refusal came from.
         (cons 'provenance
               (list (cons 'checked-at (current-time-seconds))
                     (cons 'by 'read-shop-inventory)
                     (cons 'invariant 'shop-must-be-open)))))
    (else
       ;; The happy path.
       (fetch-inventory))))

(define (shop-open?)
  (cdr (assq 'open (shoppe/balance))))

(define (fetch-inventory)
  (list (cons 'items 42) (cons 'total-value 1250.00)))

(define (current-time-seconds)
  1725609600)  ;; stand-in
```

Here is the shape. It has six parts.

How do I work with lists for depth: a taxonomy of reasons in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: catalog the reason vocabulary the ecosystem uses.
;; ROUTE: name them; note when to reach for each.

(define refusal-reasons
  '(
    ;; --- SCOPE ---
    ;; The caller is asking for something outside this verb's job.
    out-of-scope
    not-in-fork
    not-implemented-yet
    deprecated

    ;; --- PRECONDITIONS ---
    ;; A required condition is not met.
    shop-closed
    unauthenticated
    over-daily-quota
    out-of-tier
    dependency-missing
    invariant-violated

    ;; --- INPUT ---
    ;; The arguments do not make sense.
    argument-missing
    argument-malformed
    argument-out-of-range
    argument-ambiguous

    ;; --- RUNTIME ---
    ;; Something external prevented the work.
    service-not-yet-wired
    service-timed-out
    service-returned-error
    rate-limited

    ;; --- POLICY ---
    ;; The runtime or the operator has decided not to allow this.
    operator-declined
    policy-denied
    consent-not-given

    ;; --- DATA ---
    ;; The work ran but produced nothing meaningful.
    empty-result
    result-below-confidence
    ambiguous-answer

    ;; --- COMPOSITION ---
    ;; Something in the composition upstream refused.
    upstream-refused
    partial-success))

;; The vocabulary is small on purpose. When you feel tempted to invent a new
;; reason, first check whether an existing one fits. "Out-of-scope" plus a
;; specific human message covers most cases.
```

Refusal reasons are a small vocabulary. Keeping the vocabulary consistent across your codebase (and, ideally, across the whole ecosystem) is what lets callers write generic handlers.

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; A generic refusal handler an operator's script might use.
(define (handle-refusal ref)
  (let ((reason (cdr (assq 'reason ref)))
        (human (cdr (assq 'human ref)))
        (suggest (cdr (assq 'suggest ref))))
    (cond
      ((eq? reason 'shop-closed)
         (surface :say "~a Would you like to open? [y/n]" human)
         (if suggest
             (surface :say "  (~a would do it.)" suggest)))
      ((eq? reason 'over-daily-quota)
         (surface :say "~a Nothing was called." human)
         (surface :say "  Retry tomorrow, or upgrade your tier."))
      ((eq? reason 'service-not-yet-wired)
         (surface :say "~a" human)
         (surface :say "  This capability is coming; nothing to do meanwhile."))
      ((eq? reason 'argument-missing)
         (surface :say "~a Try adding the missing argument." human))
      (else
         (surface :say "Refused: ~a — ~a" reason human)))))
```

How do I meter depth: the refusal-with-retry pattern in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: a metered call is rate-limited; we refuse with a retry hint.
;; ROUTE: descriptor includes retry-after (in seconds) + a suggested wait.

(define (rate-limited-call query)
  (cond
    ((currently-rate-limited?)
       (list
         (cons 'refused #t)
         (cons 'reason 'rate-limited)
         (cons 'human "Rate limit reached for this service.")
         (cons 'retry-after 45)          ;; seconds
         (cons 'suggest '(wait-and-retry))
         (cons 'provenance
               (list (cons 'by 'rate-limited-call)
                     (cons 'seen-at (current-time-seconds))
                     (cons 'window-seconds 60)))))
    (else
       (do-the-call query))))

;; A caller-side helper that respects the retry-after.
(define (retry-when-suggested thunk max-attempts)
  (let loop ((attempt 1))
    (let ((result (thunk)))
      (cond
        ((not (refused? result))
           result)
        ((not (eq? (cdr (assq 'reason result)) 'rate-limited))
           result)
        ((>= attempt max-attempts)
           result)
        (else
           (let ((wait-sec (or (cdr (assq 'retry-after result)) 30)))
             (surface :say "Rate-limited; waiting ~a seconds (attempt ~a)."
                      wait-sec attempt)
             (sleep-seconds wait-sec)
             (loop (+ attempt 1))))))))

(define (currently-rate-limited?) #f)
(define (do-the-call q) (list (cons 'ok #t)))
(define (sleep-seconds n) #f)
```

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: a metered call is rate-limited; we refuse with a retry hint.
;; ROUTE: descriptor includes retry-after (in seconds) + a suggested wait.

(define (rate-limited-call query)
  (cond
    ((currently-rate-limited?)
       (list
         (cons 'refused #t)
         (cons 'reason 'rate-limited)
         (cons 'human "Rate limit reached for this service.")
         (cons 'retry-after 45)          ;; seconds
         (cons 'suggest '(wait-and-retry))
         (cons 'provenance
               (list (cons 'by 'rate-limited-call)
                     (cons 'seen-at (current-time-seconds))
                     (cons 'window-seconds 60)))))
    (else
       (do-the-call query))))

;; A caller-side helper that respects the retry-after.
(define (retry-when-suggested thunk max-attempts)
  (let loop ((attempt 1))
    (let ((result (thunk)))
      (cond
        ((not (refused? result))
           result)
        ((not (eq? (cdr (assq 'reason result)) 'rate-limited))
           result)
        ((>= attempt max-attempts)
           result)
        (else
           (let ((wait-sec (or (cdr (assq 'retry-after result)) 30)))
             (surface :say "Rate-limited; waiting ~a seconds (attempt ~a)."
                      wait-sec attempt)
             (sleep-seconds wait-sec)
             (loop (+ attempt 1))))))))

(define (currently-rate-limited?) #f)
(define (do-the-call q) (list (cons 'ok #t)))
(define (sleep-seconds n) #f)
```

How do I use Cortex for common desktop task: the consignment-shop operator's refusal diary in Motoi?

```motoi
;; SITUATION: end-of-week — read the refusal log, group by reason,
;;            surface the top three.
;; ROUTE: cortex read, group, sort, plot, surface.

(define (weekly-refusal-diary)
  (let ((refusals (cortex/topk
                    (list (cons 'query 'refusal-events)
                          (cons 'range 'past-7-days)
                          (cons 'k 500)))))
    (surface :say "Weekly refusal diary — ~a total refusals." (length refusals))
    (surface :say "")

    ;; Group by reason.
    (let ((grouped (group-refusals-by-reason refusals)))
      ;; Sort by count, descending.
      (let ((sorted (sort-groups-by-count grouped)))
        ;; Show the top three.
        (surface :say "Top three refusal reasons:")
        (for-each
          (lambda (group)
            (let ((reason (cdr (assq 'reason group)))
                  (count (cdr (assq 'count group)))
                  (sample (cdr (assq 'sample group))))
              (surface :say "  ~a x ~a" count reason)
              (surface :say "    example: ~a"
                       (cdr (assq 'human sample)))))
          (take sorted 3))

        ;; Plot the distribution.
        (plot/bar
          (list (cons 'series 'refusals-by-reason)
                (cons 'data grouped)))

        ;; Return the report so the caller can save or forward it.
        (list (cons 'week-of 'past-7)
              (cons 'total (length refusals))
              (cons 'by-reason sorted))))))

(define (group-refusals-by-reason refusals)
  (let ((table (make-hash-table)))
    (for-each
      (lambda (r)
        (let ((reason (cdr (assq 'reason r))))
          (let ((existing (hash-table-ref table reason
                            (lambda () (list (cons 'count 0)
                                             (cons 'sample r))))))
            (hash-table-set! table reason
              (list (cons 'reason reason)
                    (cons 'count (+ 1 (cdr (assq 'count existing))))
                    (cons 'sample (cdr (assq 'sample existing))))))))
      refusals)
    (hash-table-values table)))

(define (sort-groups-by-count groups)
  (sort groups
        (lambda (a b) (> (cdr (assq 'count a))
                         (cdr (assq 'count b))))))

(define (take lst n)
  (cond
    ((null? lst) '())
    ((zero? n) '())
    (else (cons (car lst) (take (cdr lst) (- n 1))))))

;; Missing verb note: hash-table-ref, hash-table-values, sort — these are
;; standard Scheme; if absent from the Sakura reference, they should be
;; added under core/. Missing verbs: hash-table-ref, hash-table-values,
;; sort — needs reference addition.
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of anchor: the complete closeout script.

```motoi
;; SITUATION: end-of-day closeout — seven verbs, one composition, full provenance.
;; ROUTE: guard on shop-open; sequence with refusal handling; batch metering; save.

;; -- Verb 1 -- close/gather-transactions ---------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 5 shop verb, Chapter 15 refusal shape.

(define (close/gather-transactions)
  (let ((txns (shoppe/transactions)))
    (cond
      ((null? txns)
         ;; Refusal: nothing to reconcile is a legitimate outcome.
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'empty-result)
               (cons 'human "No transactions today.")
               (cons 'suggest '(close/short-circuit))
               (cons 'provenance (list (cons 'by 'close/gather-transactions)))))
      (else
         (list (cons 'transactions txns)
               (cons 'count (length txns))
               (cons 'provenance
                     (list (cons 'source 'shoppe/transactions)
                           (cons 'at (current-time-seconds)))))))))

;; -- Verb 2 -- close/reconcile -------------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 10 adapter shape (source: shop txns; target: reconciled).
;;          Chapter 13 cross-book composition (systems + ops).

(define (close/reconcile txns)
  (let ((total (apply + (map txn-amount txns))))
    ;; Cross-check total against payments (a hypothetical payments call).
    (let ((payment-total (fetch-payment-total-today)))
      (let ((delta (- total payment-total)))
        (cond
          ((zero? delta)
             (list (cons 'reconciled #t)
                   (cons 'total total)
                   (cons 'payment-total payment-total)
                   (cons 'delta 0)
                   (cons 'provenance
                         (list (cons 'by 'close/reconcile)
                               (cons 'method 'exact-match)))))
          ((< (abs delta) 0.02)
             ;; Under two cents — probably rounding. Note but continue.
             (list (cons 'reconciled 'approx)
                   (cons 'total total)
                   (cons 'payment-total payment-total)
                   (cons 'delta delta)
                   (cons 'provenance
                         (list (cons 'by 'close/reconcile)
                               (cons 'method 'rounding-tolerance)))))
          (else
             ;; Real mismatch — refuse with the specific numbers.
             (list (cons 'refused #t)
                   (cons 'reason 'invariant-violated)
                   (cons 'human
                     (string-append "Reconciliation delta of $"
                                    (number->string delta)
                                    " — operator review needed."))
                   (cons 'suggest '(operator-review-transactions))
                   (cons 'provenance
                         (list (cons 'by 'close/reconcile)
                               (cons 'shop-total total)
                               (cons 'payment-total payment-total)
                               (cons 'delta delta))))))))))

(define (txn-amount txn)
  (cdr (assq 'amount txn)))

(define (fetch-payment-total-today)
  ;; Stand-in for a call to a payment reconciliation source.
  0.00)

;; -- Verb 3 -- close/tag-anomalies ---------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 12 metered verb; Chapter 15 refusal on quota.

(define (close/tag-anomalies txns)
  (let ((n (length txns))
        (cost-per-tag 1))
    (let ((total-cost (* n cost-per-tag)))
      ;; Ask for authorization once for the whole batch.
      (let ((auth (afford
                    (list (cons 'reason 'close-anomaly-scan)
                          (cons 'count n)
                          (cons 'total-cents total-cost)))))
        (cond
          ((not auth)
             (list (cons 'refused #t)
                   (cons 'reason 'operator-declined)
                   (cons 'human "Anomaly scan skipped; closeout continues.")
                   (cons 'suggest '(retry-with-smaller-batch))
                   (cons 'provenance
                         (list (cons 'by 'close/tag-anomalies)
                               (cons 'would-have-cost total-cost)))))
          (else
             (let ((tagged
                     (map
                       (lambda (t)
                         (let ((flag (ai/classify
                                       (list (cons 'type 'txn-anomaly)
                                             (cons 'input t)))))
                           (list (cons 'txn t)
                                 (cons 'flag flag))))
                       txns)))
               (list (cons 'tagged tagged)
                     (cons 'spent total-cost)
                     (cons 'anomaly-count
                           (length (filter (lambda (row)
                                             (not (eq? (cdr (assq 'flag row))
                                                       'normal)))
                                           tagged)))
                     (cons 'provenance
                           (list (cons 'by 'close/tag-anomalies)
                                 (cons 'method 'ai/classify)
                                 (cons 'per-tag-cost cost-per-tag))))))))))

;; -- Verb 4 -- close/summary-plot ----------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 5 shop verb, free.

(define (close/summary-plot reconcile-result tagged-result)
  (let ((total (or (cdr (assq 'total reconcile-result)) 0))
        (anomaly-count (or (cdr (assq 'anomaly-count tagged-result)) 0)))
    (plot/bar
      (list (cons 'series 'daily-closeout)
            (cons 'total total)
            (cons 'anomalies anomaly-count)))
    (list (cons 'plotted #t)
          (cons 'series 'daily-closeout)
          (cons 'provenance
                (list (cons 'by 'close/summary-plot))))))

;; -- Verb 5 -- close/save-to-cortex --------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 13 cross-book composition. Chapter 15 refusal-on-write-fail.

(define (close/save-to-cortex summary)
  (let ((write-result
          (cortex/write
            (list (cons 'key 'daily-closeout)
                  (cons 'date (today-symbol))
                  (cons 'summary summary)))))
    (cond
      ((not write-result)
         (list (cons 'refused #t)
               (cons 'reason 'service-returned-error)
               (cons 'human "Cortex write failed; summary not saved.")
               (cons 'suggest '(retry-cortex-write))
               (cons 'provenance
                     (list (cons 'by 'close/save-to-cortex)
                           (cons 'via 'cortex/write)))))
      (else
         (list (cons 'saved #t)
               (cons 'key 'daily-closeout)
               (cons 'provenance
                     (list (cons 'by 'close/save-to-cortex)
                           (cons 'via 'cortex/write))))))))

(define (today-symbol)
  ;; Stand-in — real code produces a date symbol.
  'today)

;; -- Verb 6 -- close/announce --------------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 13 cross-book composition (music + surface).

(define (close/announce)
  (radio/play 'chime-shop-closing)
  (surface/dim 0.6)
  (list (cons 'announced #t)
        (cons 'provenance
              (list (cons 'by 'close/announce)
                    (cons 'via '(radio/play surface/dim))))))

;; -- Verb 7 -- close/tomorrow-hint ---------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 12 metered (premium); Chapter 15 refusal on out-of-tier.

(define (close/tomorrow-hint)
  (let ((detail (list (cons 'query 'tomorrow-hint)
                      (cons 'context 'recent-week))))
    (let ((auth (afford
                  (list (cons 'reason 'close-tomorrow-hint)
                        (cons 'total-cents 20)))))
      (cond
        ((not auth)
           (list (cons 'refused #t)
                 (cons 'reason 'operator-declined)
                 (cons 'human "Tomorrow-hint skipped; no cost incurred.")
                 (cons 'provenance
                       (list (cons 'by 'close/tomorrow-hint)))))
        (else
           (let ((hint (afford/deep-think detail)))
             (list (cons 'hint hint)
                   (cons 'spent 20)
                   (cons 'provenance
                         (list (cons 'by 'close/tomorrow-hint)
                               (cons 'via 'afford/deep-think))))))))))

;; -- Composition -- close/run --------------------------------------------
;; PATTERN: Chapter 13 pipeline + guard + refusal cascade.
;;          Chapter 11 verb-lifecycle registration.

(define (close/run)
  ;; Guard: is the shop still marked open at closeout time? If not,
  ;; the closeout probably ran already; refuse cheaply.
  (cond
    ((shop-already-closed?)
       (list (cons 'refused #t)
             (cons 'reason 'invariant-violated)
             (cons 'human "Shop is already marked closed; closeout may have run.")
             (cons 'suggest '(check-provenance-in-cortex))
             (cons 'provenance
                   (list (cons 'by 'close/run)
                         (cons 'guard 'shop-already-closed?)))))
    (else
      ;; Step 1: gather.
      (let ((gathered (close/gather-transactions)))
        (cond
          ((and (refused? gathered)
                (eq? (cdr (assq 'reason gathered)) 'empty-result))
             ;; Short-circuit: no txns; still announce and save.
             (close/announce)
             (close/save-to-cortex '(empty-day))
             (list (cons 'closed #t)
                   (cons 'shape 'empty-day)
                   (cons 'provenance
                         (list (cons 'by 'close/run)
                               (cons 'note "No transactions; short-circuited.")))))
          ((refused? gathered)
             (cascade-refusal gathered 'close/run 'gather))
          (else
            (let ((txns (cdr (assq 'transactions gathered))))
              ;; Step 2: reconcile.
              (let ((reconcile-result (close/reconcile txns)))
                (cond
                  ((refused? reconcile-result)
                     ;; Reconciliation failed — do NOT proceed; the operator
                     ;; must review before the shop closes. This is a hard stop.
                     (cascade-refusal reconcile-result 'close/run 'reconcile))
                  (else
                    ;; Step 3: tag anomalies (metered; may be declined).
                    (let ((tagged-result (close/tag-anomalies txns)))
                      ;; Step 4: plot (free; runs regardless).
                      (let ((plot-result
                              (close/summary-plot reconcile-result tagged-result)))
                        ;; Step 5: save (may fail).
                        (let ((save-result (close/save-to-cortex
                                             (list (cons 'reconcile reconcile-result)
                                                   (cons 'tagged tagged-result)
                                                   (cons 'plot plot-result)))))
                          ;; Step 6: announce (free).
                          (close/announce)
                          ;; Step 7: tomorrow-hint (metered; may be declined).
                          (let ((hint-result (close/tomorrow-hint)))
                            ;; Compose the full provenance.
                            (list
                              (cons 'closed #t)
                              (cons 'transactions (length txns))
                              (cons 'reconcile reconcile-result)
                              (cons 'tagged tagged-result)
                              (cons 'plot plot-result)
                              (cons 'save save-result)
                              (cons 'announce (list (cons 'announced #t)))
                              (cons 'hint hint-result)
                              (cons 'provenance
                                    (list (cons 'by 'close/run)
                                          (cons 'steps '(gather reconcile tag plot save announce hint))))))))))))))))))))

(define (shop-already-closed?)
  (not (cdr (assq 'open (shoppe/balance)))))

(define (refused? x)
  (and (list? x) (assq 'refused x) (cdr (assq 'refused x))))

(define (cascade-refusal upstream by-verb at-step)
  (list
    (cons 'refused #t)
    (cons 'reason 'upstream-refused)
    (cons 'human
      (string-append (symbol->string by-verb)
                     " stopped at " (symbol->string at-step)
                     ": " (or (cdr (assq 'human upstream)) "upstream refused")))
    (cons 'suggest (cdr (assq 'suggest upstream)))
    (cons 'provenance
      (list (cons 'by by-verb)
            (cons 'stopped-at at-step)
            (cons 'upstream upstream)))))
```
